title: santalum involutum
intro: Santalum involutum, also known as the Western Australian sandalwood, is a species of tree in the sandalwood family (Santalaceae) native to Western Australia. It is prized for its fragrant and woody properties, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Santalum involutum include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as wound healing, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, teas, and tinctures, which are used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
